title: xeraea sonorae
intro: Xeraea sonorae, also known as Desert Marigold, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow or orange flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Xeraea sonorae include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
